items.prompt: A thin vertical strip of height y and width dx under a curve has elementary area dA equal to:
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch08 Sec 8.2 elementary area

items.prompt: The area bounded by y = f(x), the x-axis and the ordinates x = a, x = b equals:
items.answer: the integral of f(x) dx from a to b

expected.correct: the integral of f(x) dx from a to b
options: the integral of f(x) dx from a to b
the integral of f(x) dy from a to b

items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch08 Sec 8.2 area under a curve

items.prompt: Find the area bounded by y = x^2, the x-axis and the ordinates x = 1 and x = 2 (integral of x^2 from 1 to 2). Give the answer as a decimal.
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch08 Misc Ex Q1(i) (7/3)

items.prompt: The area bounded by x = g(y), the y-axis and the lines y = c, y = d equals:
items.answer: the integral of g(y) dy from c to d

expected.correct: the integral of g(y) dy from c to d
options: the integral of g(y) dy from c to d
the integral of g(y) dx from c to d
the integral of f(x) dx from a to b

items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch08 Sec 8.2 area w.r.t y-axis

items.prompt: If a definite integral for an area below the x-axis comes out as -5, the area of that region is:
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch08 Sec 8.2 Remark (numerical value)

items.prompt: When part of a curve is above the x-axis (area A2 > 0) and part below (A1 < 0), the total bounded area is:
Integrating to find the area enclosed by the circle x^2 + y^2 = a^2 gives:
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch08 Example 1 (area = pi a^2)

items.prompt: The area enclosed by the ellipse x^2/a^2 + y^2/b^2 = 1, found by integration, is:
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch08 Example 2 (area = pi a b)

items.prompt: Find the area bounded by y = sin x and the x-axis between x = 0 and x = 2 pi. (Take absolute values of the parts below the axis.)
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch08 Misc Ex (area of |sin x| / cos x analogue = 4)

items.prompt: Describe the general method for finding the area of a region bounded by curves, axes and lines, including how you handle parts of the region below the x-axis.

expected.rubric: Sketch the region and find the intersection / axis-crossing points to fix the limits. Choose vertical strips (integrate y dx) or horizontal strips (integrate x dy), whichever is simpler. Split the interval where the curve crosses an axis or where curves intersect, integrate each piece, and add the results - taking the absolute (numerical) value of any piece below the x-axis so positive and negative parts do not cancel.
markPoints: Sketch the region; find limits from intersections / axis crossings
Choose vertical (y dx) or horizontal (x dy) strips and integrate each piece
Add the pieces, taking absolute values of below-axis parts

items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch08 Sec 8.1/8.2 general method
